message:
fix(unistd): get_current_dir_name + tempnam use libc::malloc (bd-zgifl)

Two more external-caller-free sites fixed, continuing the bd-zgifl
sweep. Both are GNU/POSIX functions whose contract explicitly says
"caller frees with free()":

- unistd_abi::get_current_dir_name (line 9868)
- unistd_abi::tempnam               (line 10097)

Same root cause as the already-fixed host2netname / strdup / wcsdup:
raw_alloc's NATIVE_MALLOC_REENTRY bump-fallback can return pointers
outside glibc's arenas, and glibc's free cannot validate them under
thread contention → "free(): invalid size" SIGABRT.

libc::malloc resolves to our interposed malloc under LD_PRELOAD and
to glibc's malloc in non-preload test builds; either way the
caller's libc::free matches.

## Audit summary for unistd_abi's 13 raw_alloc sites

Per bd-zgifl, 13 raw_alloc call sites were enumerated in
unistd_abi.rs. Categorized:

EXTERNAL-CALLER-FREE (fixed this commit):
 - line 9868  get_current_dir_name
 - line 10097 tempnam

INTERNAL PAIRED (safe, not touched):
 - line 4907, 4927: wordexp  → freed by our wordfree → wordexp_free_wordv → raw_free
 - line 7218, 7224: getifaddrs → freed by our freeifaddrs → raw_free
 - line 16246    : __sched_cpualloc → freed by our __sched_cpufree → raw_free
 - line 3335, 3544: ftw / nftw → freed within same walk, raw_free internal
 - line 6985-7046: sigwaitinfo  → per-call scratch buffers freed in same fn

The internal-paired sites are correct because the alloc/free pair is
fully inside our own code path. A POSIX-contract-violating caller
that misuses libc::free on them would break, but that's on the
caller. No production user code would misuse those APIs because the
POSIX docs only sanction the paired free function (wordfree /
freeifaddrs / CPU_FREE).

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test -> 171 passed, 0 failed.

## bd-zgifl progress: 10 of ~30 sites fixed

Fixed: host2netname, strdup, strndup, wcsdup, scandir x3, realpath,
user2netname, get_current_dir_name, tempnam.
Remaining: wchar_abi (2 internal-use lines), inet_abi (2 internal),
string_abi (1 deferred — 5574/5593/6159/6214 all internal per my
earlier audit), deferred XDR cluster (4 sites).

All remaining sites look like INTERNAL-PAIRED cases following the
categorization pattern above, so the cross-allocator-free risk is
effectively drained from the externally-visible surface.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
